steps.title: Track the steel types
steps.content: Ferritic stainless steel, used for induction cookers, has more chromium than austenitic stainless steel. Dishwashers use 200-series austenitic stainless steel, while storage containers and sinks use 300-series austenitic stainless steel.

steps.title: Reject unsupported properties
steps.content: Choice A wrongly suggests sinks share the magnetic properties of ferritic steel. Choice B invents a chromium-threshold contrast between two austenitic subtypes. Choice C assigns the austenitic face-centered cubic structure to ferritic steel. The remaining choice follows from the chromium comparison between austenitic dishwashers and ferritic induction cookers. The answer is D.
